root_text.sn56.18:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn56.18:1.5: “Yaṁ kho, bhikkhu, dukkhe ñāṇaṁ, dukkhasamudaye ñāṇaṁ, dukkhanirodhe ñāṇaṁ, dukkhanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāya ñāṇaṁ—
root_text.sn56.18:2.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhu, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.18:0.2: 2. Rolling Forth the Wheel of Dhamma
translation_text.sn56.18:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn56.18:1.2: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.
translation_text.sn56.18:1.4: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”
translation_text.sn56.18:1.5: “Knowing about suffering, the origin of suffering, the cessation of suffering, and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.18:1.7: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.
translation_text.sn56.18:2.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
